perturbation.original_text: 1.15 "Personnel" means agents, employees, independent contractors, temporary employees or subcontractors engaged or appointed by Converge, VNI or VNE, respectively.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.15 "Personnel" means only direct employees of Converge, VNI or VNE, respectively. Temporary employees, independent contractors, and subcontractors are explicitly excluded.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Personnel' includes a broad range of individuals. The modified definition restricts 'Personnel' to only direct employees. This introduces a contradiction because other sections of the contract refer to 'Personnel' without specifying whether they mean the broad or restricted definition. This is an in-text contradiction because section 2.2 refers to authorized 'Converge Support Personnel' who may well be independent contractors and thus excluded from the amended definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Converge Support Personnel. Only authorized personnel designated by Converge ("Converge Support Personnel") may communicate Problem Reports and Support 3 Requests to VNE. The number of Converge Support Personnel will not exceed seven persons without VNE's prior approval. The initial Converge Support Personnel shall be identified to VNE in writing within 10 business days following the Effective Date. Converge may change the Converge Support Personnel on written notice to VNE; provided, however, that Converge shall use reasonable efforts not to change the Converge Support Personnel more frequently than once every 30 days. All replacement personnel shall be required to participate in Support Training as described in Section 3.3 below. Converge Support Personnel will be the "single point of contact" for the Maintenance and Support Services provided by VNE under this Section 2.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Personnel' now only includes direct employees, explicitly excluding independent contractors and subcontractors. The contradicted text, Section 2.2, refers to 'Converge Support Personnel,' who, according to the explanation, 'may well be independent contractors.' This creates a direct contradiction because if 'Converge Support Personnel' are independent contractors, they are no longer considered 'Personnel' under the new definition, making their actions and designations as 'Converge Support Personnel' questionable and potentially invalid under the contract, leading to uncertainty regarding authorized communication channels and support procedures. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' since the change creates conflicting obligations by using the term 'Personnel' with different meanings in different parts of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Response to Problem Reports. VNE shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide Converge with an initial response to and status reports for all Problems reported by Converge, and to resolve all Problems identified, in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit B attached hereto; provided, however, that VNE shall have no further obligation to respond to or attempt to diagnose or resolve a Problem once it is determined not to be attributable to Errors. VNE will review all Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level indicated by Converge unless another Problem Severity Level is clearly warranted. In the event of a reasonable uncertainty, the parties will assume a higher Severity Level for a Problem until they have sufficient information to make a determination that a lower Severity Level is warranted. If, at Converge's request, VNE responds to a Problem at a Severity Level that is higher than what proves to be the actual Severity Level of the Problem, or if VNE's review of a Problem reported by Converge establishes that the Problem was not due to an Error, Converge shall pay or reimburse VNE for all incremental fees and expenses reasonably incurred by VNE in performing such activities.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Response to Problem Reports. VNE shall use its reasonable efforts to provide Converge with an initial response to and status reports for all Problems reported by Converge, and to resolve all Problems identified, in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit B attached hereto; provided, however, that VNE shall have no further obligation to respond to or attempt to diagnose or resolve a Problem once it is determined not to be attributable to Errors. VNE will review all Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level indicated by Converge unless another Problem Severity Level is clearly warranted. In the event of a reasonable uncertainty, the parties will assume a lower Severity Level for a Problem until they have sufficient information to make a determination that a higher Severity Level is warranted. If, at Converge's request, VNE responds to a Problem at a Severity Level that is higher than what proves to be the actual Severity Level of the Problem, or if VNE's review of a Problem reported by Converge establishes that the Problem was not due to an Error, Converge shall pay or reimburse VNE for all incremental fees and expenses reasonably incurred by VNE in performing such activities.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'higher Severity Level' to 'lower Severity Level' creates an in-text contradiction within the same section. The original sentence states that in case of uncertainty, a 'higher Severity Level' is assumed until more information is available. But after the change it is contradicting to what was originally stated. This makes it unclear if 'higher' or 'lower' severity should be assumed, and thus causes uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 Response to Problem Reports. VNE shall use its reasonable efforts to provide Converge with an initial response to and status reports for all Problems reported by Converge, and to resolve all Problems identified, in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit B attached hereto; provided, however, that VNE shall have no further obligation to respond to or attempt to diagnose or resolve a Problem once it is determined not to be attributable to Errors. VNE will review all Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level indicated by Converge unless another Problem Severity Level is clearly warranted. In the event of a reasonable uncertainty, the parties will assume a lower Severity Level for a Problem until they have sufficient information to make a determination that a higher Severity Level is warranted. If, at Converge's request, VNE responds to a Problem at a Severity Level that is higher than what proves to be the actual Severity Level of the Problem, or if VNE's review of a Problem reported by Converge establishes that the Problem was not due to an Error, Converge shall pay or reimburse VNE for all incremental fees and expenses reasonably incurred by VNE in performing such activities.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that in case of reasonable uncertainty, a 'higher Severity Level' should be assumed. The changed text modifies this to say a 'lower Severity Level' should be assumed. This direct alteration creates an in-text contradiction as the two clauses now prescribe opposite actions in the same scenario, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the proper course of action.

perturbation.original_text: VNE will maintain procedures and systems designed to ensure that all Maintenance Updates are compatible with previous versions of the Supported Products and any previously provided Maintenance Updates and Enhancements.
perturbation.changed_text: VNE will attempt to maintain procedures and systems designed to ensure that all Maintenance Updates are compatible with previous versions of the Supported Products and any previously provided Maintenance Updates and Enhancements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that VNE 'will maintain' procedures and systems. By changing it to 'will attempt to maintain,' VNE's obligation is weakened from a guarantee to a 'best effort' basis. Section 2.10 states Converge agrees to install in the recommend environments all Maintenance Updates within a reasonable time following the date they are provided by VNE, taking into consideration any testing and customization required by Converge, but this change imposes a conflict. If VNE only attempts to maintain procedures and systems designed to ensure compatibility, is Converge still obligated to install the Updates within a reasonable time?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.10 Provision of Maintenance Updates. VNE shall provide Converge with Maintenance Updates as VNE makes them generally available to its other customers. Converge agrees to install in the recommend environments all Maintenance Updates within a reasonable time following the date they are provided by VNE, taking into consideration any testing and customization required by Converge (the parties expect that the time for such installation 5 generally will not exceed 90 days). Should Converge find one or more material deficiencies in any Maintenance Update, Converge shall promptly identify such deficiencies to VNE in reasonable detail.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees VNE 'will maintain' procedures, while the changed text weakens this to VNE 'will attempt to maintain'. This introduces a contradiction with Section 2.10, which requires Converge to install maintenance updates provided by VNE within a reasonable time. If VNE only attempts to maintain compatibility, the enforceability of Converge's obligation to install updates is undermined because there is now uncertainty about the updates being compatible with their environment. This creates a direct conflict and affects the obligation of Converge, making the terms inconsistent.
